pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: **Mustard Boy's Brazilian juggernaut will steamroll Ghost Pepper's misguided fantasy roster charm.** Let's get this straight—Brazil is playing against Haiti, and if anyone believes in upsets here, they need to stop playing FIFA video games. Zach, a.k.a. Mustard Boy, brilliantly took Brazil in Tier 1; you’d think he’s sat through my 1994 World Cup performances. Meanwhile, Devin, Ghost Pepper, probably just picked the USA and Norwegians because he liked their flags. The very thought that Haiti will stand a chance against Brazil is as laughable as imagining Devin cunning through a draft. I predict Brazil to win so comfortably, Devin's managerial choices will look like a clown's selection, and Mustard Boy will cruise to a decisive win.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy's Brazil Poised to Overwhelm Ghost Pepper's Haiti
pundit_takes.subtitle: Zach reportedly confident with his draft pick facing a T4 team.

pundit_takes.headline: Backpass Assassin Sees Draft Skills Backfire as Mexico Tops Korea
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden's tactical foresight questioned after Korea's scoreless defeat.

pundit_takes.headline: USA Set to Face Undrafted Australia Without Ghost Pepper's Heat
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin's strategy to rely on Spain might leave USA chilly.

pundit_takes.headline: Bubba G's Morocco to Face Scotland in Potential Revival Match
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gunner aims to climb standings with pivotal Morocco performance.

rome_analytics_quotes: Zach stuns everyone, jumps two spots to lead. Gayden, where's that 'Backpass' prowess?
Rafe's draft disaster? The kid's a chaos agent, crushing competition with spare parts.
Rafe owns Devin. Ghost Pepper can't handle a teenager—it’s a youth demolition derby!
Model loves Gunner at 26% but watches him stumble to fifth place. Embarrassing!
